meta.source: example-custom-config-master + HA Developer Docs + Jackery App RE
meta.description: Home Assistant Custom Config Examples + Complete Developer Best Practices + Jackery Integration Reference

hello_world.description: Bare minimum sync component - sets a state

hello_world_async.description: Bare minimum async component - sets a state

example_light.description: Light integration with external library (awesomelights), YAML config, polling

light.py.platform_schema: PLATFORM_SCHEMA.extend({vol.Required(CONF_HOST): cv.string, vol.Optional(CONF_USERNAME, default='admin'): cv.string, vol.Optional(CONF_PASSWORD): cv.string})

example_light.config_yaml:
light:
  - platform: example_light
    host: HOST_HERE
    username: USERNAME_HERE
    password: PASSWORD_HERE

example_load_platform.description: Component that loads its own platform and shares data via hass.data
patterns_demonstrated: data sharing between component and platform

expose_service_sync.description: Sync component that registers a custom service

expose_service_async.description: Async component that registers a custom service with CONFIG_SCHEMA

mqtt_basic_sync.description: Sync MQTT component - subscribe and publish

mqtt_basic_async.description: Async MQTT component - subscribe and publish

detailed_hello_world_push.description: Comprehensive async PUSH integration with config flow, cover + sensors, device registry, callbacks

Hub_class.description: Dummy hub for Hello World example

Roller_class.delayed_update: async def delayed_update() with random delay

counter.description: Simple counter that increments on each script run

external_library_requirements: All communication to external devices/services must be wrapped in external Python library hosted on PyPI
Library must have source distribution packages available (no binary-only packages)
Issue trackers must be enabled for external libraries
New dependencies added to requirements_all.txt via: python3 -m script.gen_requirements_all
New codeowners added to CODEOWNERS via: python3 -m script.hassfest
.strict-typ file updated if fully type hinted

common: Only add new constants to const.py if widely used
external_requirements: Requirements added to manifest.json (REQUIREMENTS constant deprecated)
configuration: Voluptuous schema present for configuration validation
Default parameters specified in voluptuous schema, not in setup()
Schema using generic config keys from homeassistant.const
If component has platforms, define PLATFORM_SCHEMA instead of CONFIG_SCHEMA
Never depend on users adding things to customize
communication: Share data with platforms via hass.data[DOMAIN]
Use dispatcher code in homeassistant.helpers.dispatcher for updates
device_communication: All API specific code must be part of third party library hosted on PyPI
Home Assistant should only interact with objects, not make direct API calls
GOOD: from phue import Bridge; bridge = Bridge(...); status = bridge.status()

setup: Group calls to add_entities if possible
entity: Extend entity from integration being built for
Avoid passing hass as parameter (set when entity added to HA)
Do not call update() in constructor, use add_entities(devices, update_before_add=True)
Do not do I/O inside properties, cache values in update()
Store UTC timestamps, not relative time

authentication.purpose: Acquire authentication tokens and make authenticated requests
requirements: Should acquire tokens, update if necessary, use for requests
Return authentication in JSON serializable format (dict with primitive types)
Allow developer to pass API location if served from multiple locations

Auth.__init__: websession: ClientSession, host: str, access_token: str
Auth.request: async def request(method: str, path: str, **kwargs) -> ClientResponse
def request(method: str, path: str, **kwargs) -> requests.Response

oauth2.description: Standardized authentication using refresh and access tokens

async_pattern.implementation: Developer implements async_get_access_token with token manager

Auth.request: def request(method: str, path: str, **kwargs) -> Response

principles: Expose data in same structure as API offers
Do not transform data (e.g., no Celsius/Fahrenheit conversion)
Let developer using library handle transformations

structure.properties: Map to API field names (id, name, is_on)

basic_library_requirements: Versions correspond to tagged releases in public repository
Publishing on PyPI must be automated
OSI-approved license for library and subdependencies

required_fields.domain: Short name with characters and underscores, must be unique, matches directory
required_fields.name: Integration name (append 'Cloud' for cloud variants, no suffix for local)

optional_fields.version: Required for custom integrations (CalVer or SemVer)
optional_fields.dependencies: Other integrations that must be set up first
optional_fields.after_dependencies: Optional dependencies that might be used
optional_fields.config_flow: true if integration has config flow
optional_fields.single_config_entry: true if only one config entry allowed
optional_fields.loggers: List of logger names used by requirements

rules: Name property identifies data point, not device or entity type
Main feature of device: name = None
friendly_name = device.name + entity.name (or just device.name if entity.name is None)

methods: Entity description: entity_description attribute with EntityDescription instance

lifecycle_hooks.async_added_to_hass: Called when entity_id and hass assigned, before first write
lifecycle_hooks.async_will_remove_from_hass: Called when entity about to be removed

recorder_exclusions.description: Exclude attributes not suitable for history recording

best_practices: Add bluetooth_adapters to dependencies in manifest.json
Call bluetooth.async_get_scanner API to get BleakScanner instance
Use connection timeout of at least 10 seconds
Use bleak-retry-connector package for reliable connections

connectable_modes.connectable_false: Device only needs advertisement data, receives from all controllers

PassiveBluetoothProcessorCoordinator.use_case: Primary method is BLE advertisements, primary function is sensor/binary_sensor/events
PassiveBluetoothProcessorCoordinator.description: Minimal code needed, frameworks create entities on demand

ActiveBluetoothProcessorCoordinator.description: Like Passive but with needs_poll_method and poll_method

PassiveBluetoothCoordinator.description: Similar to DataUpdateCoordinator but driven by advertisements

ActiveBluetoothDataUpdateCoordinator.use_case: Active BLE connections, not primarily sensor
ActiveBluetoothDataUpdateCoordinator.description: Like Passive but with polling capability

DataUpdateCoordinator.use_case: Device only uses active BLE connection, no advertisements

device_tracking.async_track_unavailable: Get callback when device no longer seen
device_tracking.async_scanner_devices_by_address: Get devices by address from all adapters

external_scanners.async_get_advertisement_callback: Get callback to feed advertisement data

performance_considerations.match_history: Do not clear for frequently changing data (sensors)
performance_considerations.advertisement_history: Clear after GATT session ends to receive wake-up advertisements

show_form.sections: vol.Schema with section() for grouping fields

show_menu.menu_options: List or dict of step_id: label

validation.description: Validate user input after schema validation
validation.errors: Dict with field names as keys, translation keys as values
validation.base_errors: Use 'base' key for errors unrelated to specific field

multi_step_flows.pattern: Store info in self, return await self.async_step_next()

yaml_import.note: New integrations should not accept credentials in configuration.yaml

lookup_helpers.async_get_discovery_info_by_udn_st: Single device by UDN and ST

network.returns: List of adapter dicts with ipv4/ipv6 info

usb.async_is_plugged_in: Check if specific USB adapter present
usb.async_register_scan_request_callback: Callback when new USB devices available

authentication_providers.description: Confirm user identity via login flow
authentication_providers.note: Currently only built-in auth providers supported

features: Establish connection and authenticate with HA instance
Call service actions, fire events, render templates
View to control house via authenticated webview

server_states: 4. Command phase (client sends commands, server sends results)

entity_registry_display.description: Lightweight list of entity registry entries for UI display

get_events.description: List event types with listener count

services_api.description: Internal services API for service discovery

significant_change.description: Detect significant state changes for entities

reproduce_state.use_case: Restore entity states when activating scenes

raising_exceptions.translation_support: Exceptions can include translation_key, translation_domain, translation_placeholders
raising_exceptions.logging: ServiceValidationError logs at debug level only

repairs.fixable: True if issue can be fixed via repair flow

development_typing.description: Type hints for better code quality
best_practices: Use type hints for all function parameters and return values
Use Union for multiple possible types
Use TYPE_CHECKING for imports only needed for type checking
Run mypy for static type checking

parameters.require_current_request: bool - Require URL to match current request
parameters.allow_cloud: bool - Allow Home Assistant Cloud URLs

instance_url.exception: NoURLAvailableError if no suitable URL found

logger_management.warning: KRITISCH: Logger NICHT löschen ohne Code vorher zu prüfen
logger_management.best_practice: Logger beibehalten, nur Log-Level anpassen wenn nötig

required_fields.domain: Short name with underscores, unique, matches directory
required_fields.name: Integration name (append 'Cloud' for cloud variants)

optional_fields.single_config_entry: true if only one entry allowed
optional_fields.dependencies: Other integrations that must be set up first

unique_id.requirements: Must be stable string, not user-changeable

config_entries.description: Configuration data persistently stored by Home Assistant

lifecycle_states.not_loaded: Initial state when created or HA restarted
lifecycle_states.setup_retry: Dependency not ready, will retry automatically

unloading.cleanup: Clean up entities, unsubscribe listeners, close connections

subentries.description: Logically separate configuration data within entry
subentries.example: Weather forecasts: auth in entry, locations as subentries

offline_device.logging: Only debug level, rely on built-in logic

expired_credentials.location: Must be from async_setup_entry in __init__.py or DataUpdateCoordinator

bronze.description: Baseline standard for all new integrations

properties.no_io_in_properties: Cache values in update(), properties read from memory only
properties.utc_timestamps: Store UTC timestamps, not relative time

authentication.json_serializable: Return auth as dict with primitive types

principles: Expose data in same structure as API
Do not transform data (no unit conversions)

validation.errors_dict: Field names as keys, translation keys as values

connection: Use timeout of at least 10 seconds

passive_processor.benefits: Minimal code, frameworks create entities on demand

active_scan.requires: address in matcher and mode != PASSIVE

scanner_management.warning: Scanner objects are read-only, never modify

overview.description: Built-in authentication system for users to interact with Home Assistant

authentication_providers.credential_sharing: Providers of same type don't share credentials
authentication_providers.built_in: Home Assistant provider (stores users in config directory)

credentials.description: Store authentication of user with specific provider
credentials.user_creation: If user doesn't exist, created but needs approval
credentials.multiple_credentials: User can have multiple, but only one per provider

users.owner: User created during onboarding, can manage others, has all permissions

permissions.description: Permission policy describes group access to resources

tokens.access_token: Used to access HA APIs, limited lifetime
tokens.refresh_token: Used to get new access token, valid until deleted

types.long_lived: Back long-lived access tokens, created internally
types.system: Limited to system users (HA OS, Supervisor)

auth_provider_implementation.note: Currently only built-in auth providers supported

best_practices: Import and extend PLATFORM_SCHEMA from integration
Preferred order: required first, optional second
Default values must be valid (not None)

development_typing.description: Type hints for better code understanding
development_typing.purpose: Static annotations for variables and functions
development_typing.coverage: Not required for all modules, but encouraged
development_typing.ci_check: All code type checked in CI
development_typing.strict_typing: Add module to .strict-typing file for strict checks
development_typing.note: Most useful for completely untyped modules

instance_url.description: Get URL of user's Home Assistant instance

parameters.prefer_cloud: Prefer cloud over user external (default False)

instance_url.default_behavior: Prefer local, allow any, no requirements
instance_url.exception: NoURLAvailableError when no URL matches requirements

significant_change.description: Filter insignificant changes for external services
significant_change.automatic_handling: unknown and unavailable handled by HA

reproduce_state.description: Support for scenes - reproduce entity states
reproduce_state.purpose: Call right service actions to achieve states

raising_exceptions.description: Proper exception handling in service actions
raising_exceptions.logging: ServiceValidationError only logs at debug level

repairs.description: Track issues to bring to user's attention

attributes.issue_domain: Set when creating on behalf of another

persistence.is_persistent_true: Shown again after restart (for detection-time issues)
persistence.is_persistent_false: Not shown after restart until recreated (for checkable issues)

ignored_issues.lifecycle: Ignored until deleted by integration or successful repair

device_automation.warning: Exploring alternatives, new device automations won't be accepted

conditions.description: Check if condition is met (e.g., light on, floor wet)

triggers.description: Automation triggers tied to device (e.g., light turned on, water detected)

actions.description: Have device do something (e.g., turn light on, open door)

features: Call service actions, fire events, render templates

methods: Ask user to add mobile_app to configuration.yaml

encryption.secret_storage: Store forever, cannot recover via UI

updating.is_disabled: Present if entity disabled in HA

notifications.description: Push notifications via websocket or cloud

cloud.enable: Set push_token and push_url in app_data
cloud.restart_required: User must restart HA after setting keys

documentation_standards.style_guide: Microsoft Style Guide + HA style guide
documentation_standards.deprecated_features: Remove documentation, do not add deprecation notices

logger_management.warning: KRITISCH: Logger NICHT löschen ohne Code vorher zu prüfen
logger_management.issue: Gelöschte Logger verhindern Fehlerdiagnose in Logs

setup_failures.config_entry_not_ready: Nur in __init__.py async_setup_entry werfen, NICHT in platform async_setup_entry
setup_failures.logging: Nur debug-level für Retries, kein Spam
